title: Build a basic LED circuit
description: Learn the basics of circuits by wiring an LED with a current-limiting resistor on a breadboard.

dialogue.text: Hey there! I'm Orion. Ready to see how simple electronics can be? We'll wire a 5 mm LED with a 220 Ω resistor on a breadboard and keep things safe.

Gather a breadboard, two jumper wires, a 5 mm LED, a 220 Ω resistor and a 5 V power supply (battery pack or USB adapter). Keep the supply unplugged while wiring; the resistor protects the LED from too much current. Ready to assemble?

Insert the LED into the breadboard with the long leg (anode) on the positive rail. Place the 220 Ω resistor from the anode's row to a new row, then use jumper wires to link the resistor to +5 V and the LED's short leg (cathode) to ground. Confirm the resistor is in series and polarity is correct before applying power. When it all looks secure, connect the 5 V power supply and the LED should glow.
Great job! You've built your first circuit and lit up an LED. Always disconnect power before changing any connections.
